steps.title: Translate the problem to equations
steps.content:
Let $p$ = pounds of peaches and $c$ = pounds of cucumbers.

From the problem statement:
  • Cucumber quantity: $c = 2p$ (twice as many pounds as peaches)
  • Total cost equation: $0.75p + 1.10c = 8.85$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for pounds of peaches
steps.content:
Substitute $c = 2p$ into the cost equation:
$$0.75p + 1.10(2p) = 8.85$$
Combine the $p$ terms:
$$0.75p + 2.20p = 8.85$$
$$2.95p = 8.85$$
Divide both sides by 2.95:
$$p = \frac{8.85}{2.95} = 3$$

steps.title: Calculate the pounds of cucumbers
steps.content:
Using the relationship $c = 2p$ with $p = 3$:
$$c = 2(3) = 6 \text{ pounds}$$
Verification: $0.75(3) + 1.10(6) = 2.25 + 6.60 = 8.85$, so the solution checks.

D is correct.
